Learning Objective: Introduce Curiosity Explorers and the BCube discovery journey.
Illustration Direction: A unique explorer classroom scene with Star and diverse children using magnifying glasses to observe leaves, butterflies, shells and simple science objects.

Learning Objective: Present complete publisher and edition information.
Illustration Direction: Clean legal-information page with small approved brand elements and no decorative clutter.

Learning Objective: Welcome the child into an observation-and-discovery journey.
Illustration Direction: Star welcomes diverse preschool children into a bright discovery corner with magnifying glass, leaf, shell and butterfly.

Learning Objective: Show adults and children the recurring activity symbols and interaction flow.
Illustration Direction: Four large visual steps: look, think, explore and talk, each represented by one clear icon and a child action.

Learning Objective: Introduce Star as a consistent inquiry companion.
Illustration Direction: Approved Star mascot holding a magnifying glass with four simple fact callouts: looks carefully, asks questions, explores safely, shares discoveries.

Learning Objective: Build safe and positive exploration habits.
Illustration Direction: Four promise panels with a child observing, asking, exploring safely and caring for nature; include large traceable key words.

Learning Objective: Find named objects by scanning a simple scene carefully.
Illustration Direction: A simple playground scene containing a butterfly, ball, bird, kite, flower, bench and tree; Star observes from the side with a magnifying glass.

Learning Objective: Compare familiar objects by size.
Illustration Direction: Four pairs of familiar objects with obvious size contrast: balls, teddy bears, cups and leaves.

Learning Objective: Identify one object that differs from a small group.
Illustration Direction: Three rows of four large objects; one item in each row differs by colour, shape or orientation.

Learning Objective: Match identical objects using visual discrimination.
Illustration Direction: Two columns of six large familiar objects in shuffled order with generous line-drawing space.

Learning Objective: Recall and identify a missing object in a simple sequence.
Illustration Direction: Three short visual sequences with one blank and two large answer choices beneath each.

Learning Objective: Notice small changes between two related scenes.
Illustration Direction: Two side-by-side preschool garden scenes with exactly three obvious differences and no tiny details.

Learning Objective: Count visible objects from one to five.
Illustration Direction: Five groups of large nature objects, quantities one through five, with large numeral choices.

Learning Objective: Review size, matching, difference and counting skills.
Illustration Direction: Four clearly separated mini-activities using familiar objects from pages 7–13.

Learning Objective: Notice differences in leaf shape, size and edge.
Illustration Direction: Four large leaf types with distinct silhouettes and one simple tree reference.

Learning Objective: Observe flower colour and petal count.
Illustration Direction: Four large simple flowers with clearly separated petals and friendly garden accents.

Learning Objective: Identify the main visible parts of a tree.
Illustration Direction: One large child-friendly tree showing roots, trunk, branches and leaves with four label cards.

Learning Objective: Recognize familiar fruits and connect them with colours.
Illustration Direction: Apple, banana, orange, grapes and watermelon shown as large isolated objects with colour swatches.

Learning Objective: Recognize familiar vegetables and sort them into a basket.
Illustration Direction: Carrot, tomato, potato, brinjal, peas and cucumber arranged around one large basket.

Learning Objective: Observe common bird features and identify a nest.
Illustration Direction: Three friendly birds, one nest, feathers and eggs in a simple outdoor setting.

Learning Objective: Compare two familiar garden insects.
Illustration Direction: One large butterfly and one large bee with simple comparison icons for wings, colours and movement.

Learning Objective: Recognize sunny, rainy, cloudy and windy weather.
Illustration Direction: Four equal weather panels with one child dressed appropriately in each.

Learning Objective: Recognize common farm animals and their sounds.
Illustration Direction: Cow, sheep, horse, hen and duck as large friendly illustrations with sound cards.

Learning Objective: Distinguish wild animals from familiar domestic animals.
Illustration Direction: Lion, elephant, tiger and giraffe mixed with dog and cat, all in separate clear frames.

Learning Objective: Identify animals that live in water.
Illustration Direction: Fish, dolphin, octopus, turtle and starfish mixed with two land animals.

Learning Objective: Recognize common birds and connect them to the sky.
Illustration Direction: Parrot, pigeon, crow and sparrow with one clear sky area and simple matching paths.

Learning Objective: Match common animals with their homes.
Illustration Direction: Dog and kennel, bird and nest, rabbit and burrow, bee and hive in two aligned columns.

Learning Objective: Match animal babies with their parents.
Illustration Direction: Cow-calf, dog-puppy, cat-kitten and hen-chick as four clear pairs.

Learning Objective: Connect familiar animals with foods they eat.
Illustration Direction: Rabbit-carrot, monkey-banana, cow-grass and bird-seeds with large recognizable objects.

Learning Objective: Review animal groups, homes, babies and foods.
Illustration Direction: Four mini-activities using only animals introduced on pages 23–29.

Learning Objective: Recognize familiar rooms and household objects.
Illustration Direction: Four simple room panels: bedroom, kitchen, bathroom and living room, with one movable object card each.

Learning Objective: Recognize familiar places and people at school.
Illustration Direction: School entrance, classroom, playground and teacher shown in four balanced panels.

Learning Objective: Find and name common classroom objects.
Illustration Direction: Clean classroom scene containing chair, table, book, pencil, bag, board and blocks.

Learning Objective: Observe playground equipment and safe play actions.
Illustration Direction: Children using slide, swing and seesaw safely, with one bench, tree and ball.

Learning Objective: Match community helpers with their tools.
Illustration Direction: Doctor, firefighter, teacher and police officer with stethoscope, hose, book and traffic baton.

Learning Objective: Sort transport by land, water and air.
Illustration Direction: Car, bus, bicycle, boat, ship, aeroplane and helicopter with three large category zones.

Learning Objective: Classify familiar objects by where they are usually found.
Illustration Direction: Bed, sofa and lamp mixed with tree, swing and garden ball; two large labelled zones.

Learning Objective: Follow a simple route and recognize neighbourhood landmarks.
Illustration Direction: Very simple map with home, school, park and shop linked by one wide dotted route.

Learning Objective: Compare familiar objects by likely weight.
Illustration Direction: Four object pairs with obvious weight contrast, such as feather-rock and balloon-watermelon.

Learning Objective: Classify familiar objects by texture.
Illustration Direction: Pillow, cotton, teddy, stone, wooden block and spoon with two large category areas.

Learning Objective: Make simple predictions about objects in water.
Illustration Direction: Clear water tub with leaf, stone, cork, spoon and toy boat shown beside two answer columns.

Learning Objective: Review comparison, classification and prediction.
Illustration Direction: Four mini-activities reviewing heavy-light, soft-hard and sink-float with large objects.

Learning Objective: Celebrate completion and reinforce explorer identity.
Illustration Direction: Premium certificate with approved Star, magnifying-glass emblem, child-name line, date and teacher signature line.

Learning Objective: Encourage continued observation, questioning and safe exploration.
Illustration Direction: Back cover with Star and children observing nature; four pillar badges only: Creativity, Communication, Curiosity and Confidence, without descriptions.
